payload.text:
You are Sudarshan CRM Assistant.
You help Indian MSMEs, shop-owners, retailers and service providers manage their basic CRM and accounting — using Hindi, English or Hinglish naturally.

🎯 Your Core Responsibilities

Understand user messages in Hindi, English, or Hinglish

Identify the user’s intent

Convert every message into a structured JSON action object that the backend can execute

Help create and manage customer invoices

When the user wants to share an invoice, also generate ready-to-send message text for:

Gmail

WhatsApp

Keep your tone simple, clear, polite and business-friendly.

🧠 NEW FEATURE — DAILY HANDWRITTEN DIARY IMAGE ANALYSIS

Users may upload a photo of their handwritten diary / notebook, where they have written daily business hisaab such as:

sale, sales, bikri, aamdani

purchase, kharid, stock, samaan liya

expense, kharcha, rent, chai-paani, diesel, helper, light bill, stall rent

🔍 What you must do

When an image of handwritten entries is given:

1️⃣ Carefully read the handwriting (Hindi, English or Hinglish).
2️⃣ Extract each transaction line and classify it as one of:

"SALE" (income)

"PURCHASE" (goods/stock bought)

"EXPENSE" (other expenses)

3️⃣ For every extracted entry, return:

type: "SALE" | "PURCHASE" | "EXPENSE"

label: short description such as "Sabzi bikri", "Mandi se kharid", "Stall rent"

amount: number in rupees

4️⃣ Calculate totals:

total_sale

total_purchase

total_expense

profit_loss = total_sale - (total_purchase + total_expense)

5️⃣ Provide:

A short & simple Hindi summary

Exactly 5 action-oriented suggestions in Hindi
explaining how the shopkeeper can improve the next day.

Use very simple, everyday Hindi — the kind that a vegetable vendor, kirana shop owner, or small business owner can easily understand. {{ The Detailed Steps and Information , Features are Given in the attachment. Analyse the attachment and Strictly Follow the Instructions.}}

**Designing the React App**

I'm currently focused on the React application architecture for the "Sudarshan CRM Assistant." The target audience is Indian MSMEs, shop-owners, and retailers, so I'm thinking about how to best structure the application to support Hindi, English, and Hinglish. My core tasks revolve around efficient customer, invoice, and payment management.


**Prioritizing Core Actions**

I've been working on the action contracts, making sure they're clear and complete.  The focus is still on the core actions: customer management, balance retrieval, payments, invoicing, and diary analysis. I'm focusing on defining the intent-to-JSON action conversion. Shareable message generation is also on the radar. The UI design will be India-first: simple, fast, and mobile-friendly.


**Outlining App Components**

I'm now diving into the component breakdown and file structure. Key files are shaping up: `index.html`, `index.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and `types.ts` for JSON action types and transactions. I'm focusing on the `services/gemini.ts` file for API calls and the `components/DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` component, which is the core. I'm also planning the bottom navigation for quick access and a design with clean cards, shadows, and indigo/violet primary colors for a modern, professional feel.


**Building Core Functionality**

I'm deep into component development, focusing on critical elements. I've defined data structures like `CrmAction`, `Transaction`, and `Insight`. The app structure centers on `App.tsx` for layout and routing, and `gemini.ts` which handles the API calls for both image analysis and chat processing. I'm building a simple 5-step Hindi flow for actions and working on profit/loss calculations from transactions categorized as "SALE," "PURCHASE," or "EXPENSE." The `ANALYZE_DAILY_TRANSACTIONS` response format is now my priority, especially for the Hindi ledger summary.

payload.text:
For each customer in the Customer Manager, make the 'View Ledger' button functional. This should navigate the user to a detailed view of that specific customer's transactions and balance. {Add a "Dashboard Screen".} Also add below features.
Make UI better

**Developing Ledger Functionality**

I'm currently focused on the "View Ledger" button within the `CustomerManager`. My priority is establishing seamless navigation to a comprehensive customer detail view. This involves connecting the button's action to a new detailed display.


**Analyzing Customer Data**

I've been drilling into the customer data, specifically focusing on building out interfaces for transaction history and detailed customer information in `types.ts`. I also shifted the `analyzeDiaryImage` model to `gemini-3-pro-preview` for image analysis in `services/gemini.ts`, and optimized `processChat` to `gemini-3-flash-preview`. Furthermore, I added state management in `App.tsx` for navigating to ledger views and created the initial components for displaying customer ledgers.


**Improving Dashboard Display**

I'm now zeroing in on the dashboard screen, planning to enrich it with a "Daily Summary" card and improve the overall visual appeal using Tailwind. The goal is a more interactive and modern interface. Also, I'm integrating the ledger view, showing customer details and recent transactions along with key actions like adding payments or creating invoices. I'm still using `gemini-3-pro-preview` for image analysis because it's a complex task, as I decided based on the prompt's instructions.

payload.text: On the Customer Ledger screen, add quick action buttons for 'Add Sale', 'Add Purchase', and 'Add Expense' that pre-fill the customer name.Analyse both attachments and create dashboard similar to the design & style. Also Customise it to Indian Currency , & items.  Add Gemini Intelligence & Hindi Voice capability

**Considering Design Changes**

I'm currently focused on the dark theme implementation for the Sudarshan CRM Assistant. I'm exploring how to best integrate this design aesthetic across the application, keeping functionality intact. I've begun reviewing the existing code to identify areas needing modification, prioritizing visual elements for the immediate overhaul.


**Refining Dashboard Analytics**

I'm now prioritizing the analytics dashboard design, aiming for impactful visualizations. I'm focusing on "Total Sales," "Visitors," "Conversion Rate," "Sales Overview," and other key metrics, and implementing appropriate chart types (line, donut, bar). Indian-specific localization is underway, too.


**Designing UI Elements**

My focus has shifted to the granular UI elements. I'm prioritizing rounded corners, shadows, and subtle gradients for visual appeal. Progress bars will be integrated directly into stat cards. For charts, I'm leaning toward CSS or SVG-based solutions for efficiency and customization, steering clear of heavy libraries. I have to make sure to update the index.html, App.tsx, Dashboard.tsx, CustomerLedger.tsx, ChatAgent.tsx, and services/gemini.ts files.


**Adding Hindi Language Support**

I'm now implementing Hindi language support for AI responses within the Sudarshan CRM Assistant, focusing on immediate integration for broader accessibility. I'm also working on context-aware actions triggered by the ledger buttons to streamline user workflows, enhancing the application's overall responsiveness.

payload.text:
In the Customer Ledger screen, implement the 'Add Sale', 'Add Purchase', and 'Add Expense' quick action buttons. When clicked, they should pre-fill the customer name and allow the user to input the transaction details.Implement the 'Add' button functionality in the Customer Manager. This should open a modal or a new view to add a new customer with fields for business name, owner name, and phone number.Implement functionality for the 'WhatsApp' and 'Share PDF' buttons on the Customer Ledger screen. For WhatsApp, pre-fill a message with the customer's outstanding balance. For PDF, generate a summary of the customer's transactions.Apply the edits shown in the screenshot(s). Make Texts more Visible. Also Add " Simple KANBAN BOARD"
 in simple language to assign leads & tasks using that. Give it a Vibrant & indian UI style. It should be very encouraging and attractive UI. Add Whsatsapp & Gmail options also , where user can Login and use these tools. Also scan and rectify the flow of "Scan & Calculate analysis}.

**Initiating Refinements**

I'm now zeroing in on the `Sudarshan CRM Assistant` app, specifically its customer ledger. My focus is on integrating quick action buttons for adding sales, purchases, and expenses directly within the ledger view. I'm thinking of using modal windows or similar views for capturing the transaction details efficiently.


**Implementing Integration Features**

I'm now integrating WhatsApp and Gmail features. I'll add buttons to share balance via WhatsApp and PDF transaction summaries. Plus, I'll enhance UI to improve text visibility and align with the existing dark theme and vibrant Indian style. I'm also preparing to add a simple Kanban board for leads and tasks, using simple language.


**Designing New Components**

Currently, I'm developing new components, with an emphasis on creating the `KanbanBoard.tsx` for leads/tasks and enhancing the flow in `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` for clearer steps and better feedback. I'm also preparing to update the `CustomerLedger.tsx` to handle adding transactions, plus I'm planning out the new modal for adding customers in `CustomerManager.tsx`.


**Prioritizing Workflow States**

My focus has shifted to the Kanban board, specifically the naming conventions. I've translated key workflow stages into Hindi: "Naye Log" for new leads, "Baat Chal Rahi Hai" for in-progress interactions, and "Deal Pakki" for completed deals. Now it's time to build it!

entries.description: Add types for Leads/Kanban and update transaction handling.
New vibrant Indian-style Kanban board for leads and tasks.
Add 'Add Customer' modal and improve text visibility.
Implement quick actions, communication tools (WhatsApp/PDF), and better visibility.
Refined flow for scanning and analysis with clearer instructions and visual cues.
Integrate the Kanban board and handle tab switching for the new module.
Update navigation to include Leads/Kanban.

payload.text: Enhance the overall UI of the application with a more vibrant and attractive Indian aesthetic, ensuring texts are more visible and the interface is encouraging. Then , Integrate a simple Kanban board for assigning leads and tasks, using encouraging and attractive UI elements with an Indian style. Add Generative AI features.

**Exploring Design Options**

I've been mulling over the UI/UX enhancements for the "Sudarshan CRM Assistant." The Indian aesthetic is shaping up nicely, and I'm exploring vibrant color palettes. Increased visibility is another major focus, and I'm considering font choices and layout options. The Kanban board is also progressing, with thought being given to its precise layout and functionality. I have begun to understand how Gemini integration will work.


**Refining Model Integration**

I'm now fully immersed in the model integration. I'm focusing on the AI chatbot, implementing `gemini-3-pro-preview` for sophisticated Q&A in `ChatAgent.tsx`. Simultaneously, I'm developing a voice app, potentially `VoiceAssistant.tsx`, with `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025` for live voice interactions. I'm also adding `microphone` permissions for the Live API in `metadata.json` and refining the model selection in `services/gemini.ts`.


**Finalizing Component Integrations**

I've been integrating the `VoiceAssistant.tsx` and `ChatAgent.tsx` components. I'm leveraging `gemini-3-pro-preview` for the chatbot and `gemini-2.5-flash-native-audio-preview-09-2025` for the live audio API. I'm implementing the necessary audio processing functions for `VoiceAssistant` in preparation for handling live voice interactions, and I've started the color and UI implementation of the Kanban board, and have begun adding the new Voice Assistant to the `App.tsx` file.


**Combining Mode Integration**

I'm now consolidating the chatbot and voice app functionalities. The plan is to either merge them under an "Assistant" tab or introduce a distinct "Live" mode. This streamlines user interaction and simplifies the UI. I'll need to refactor the initial setup to reflect this merged approach.

entries.description: Added microphone permission for Gemini Live API audio interaction.
Updated theme colors and added a subtle Indian pattern background for a more vibrant UI.
Updated to use gemini-3-pro-preview for complex chat tasks as requested.

Refined Chat UI with better visibility, larger text, and vibrant Indian accents.
Enhanced Kanban Board with vibrant Indian-inspired colors and better visibility.
Added Voice mode to the main navigation and unified the assistant experience.
Updated navigation bar with Live Voice option and polished vibrant styling.

payload.text: Add a feature to set reminders for specific leads on the Kanban board. Allow users to edit the status of existing leads directly within the Kanban board. Allow users to edit the status of existing leads directly within the Kanban board. Enable assigning leads to different team members within the Kanban board interface.

**Exploring KanbanBoard Enhancements**

I'm now focusing on improving the KanbanBoard in my Sudarshan CRM. I'm exploring adding reminders for leads directly within the board. While direct status editing exists, I'm thinking of a more streamlined approach, perhaps in-line editing. I'm also considering other optimizations to workflow.


**Integrating Lead Assignments**

I've just added reminder date and assignee fields to the Lead interface in `types.ts`. Now, I'm working on `KanbanBoard.tsx`. My current focus is implementing a user-friendly UI for lead assignments. The plan is to add a pencil icon to each card for inline editing, offering a date picker for reminders and a simple selection list with names like "Ravi", "Meena", and "Self".  I'm striving to keep the Indian-styled UI intact while integrating these features seamlessly. The status editing should also remain intuitive.


**Developing Modal Integration**

I'm now integrating an "Edit Lead" modal to streamline lead management. It will consolidate status changes, reminder setting, and team member assignments within a single interface, starting with the `types.ts` modifications. I anticipate this will improve the Sudarshan CRM's lead management workflow by offering a centralized hub for necessary adjustments.

entries.description: Add reminderDate and assignedTo fields to the Lead interface.
Update KanbanBoard with lead reminders, assignee management, and status editing via a new edit modal.

payload.text: Apply the edits shown in the screenshot(s). The cards are not sliding and changing , also check for the Optimisation of the app for all Mobile screens. Add Home Page and Hindi option also.

**Refining the UI**

I'm focusing now on the UI changes requested, particularly how to integrate the horizontal scrolling Kanban board ("Kaam Ki List"). I'm exploring different layouts and component options to achieve the desired look and feel. I am giving priority to the overall user experience and how to make the UI intuitive. I'm considering using a scrolling container with some custom scrollbar styling.


**Integrating Improvements**

I'm now prioritizing implementing the touch-friendly horizontal scroll on the Kanban board using `snap-x snap-mandatory` and `overflow-x-auto`. I'll integrate a dedicated Home page within the existing Dashboard component and add a language state in `App.tsx` for easy switching between English and Hindi. Responsive design for mobile is another important consideration.


**Addressing Mobile Layout Concerns**

I'm now prioritizing mobile-first design, specifically how to ensure horizontal scrolling doesn't disrupt the layout on smaller screens. I am making updates to the `App.tsx` file for language state and BottomNav labels. The Dashboard is being updated as the homepage, now with localized content, and I'll finish localizing the KanbanBoard. Everything should feel high-quality, with a dark theme and vibrant colors, and the Kanban scroll must be perfect. I'll need to check the screenshot again.


**Revising Card Behavior**

I'm now focusing on the horizontal scroll and card interactions on the Kanban board. The feedback suggests some issues with the sliding and changing behavior, so I am refining it. I'll need to double-check the integration. I'll begin implementing the language toggle and update the `App.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, `BottomNav.tsx`, and `KanbanBoard.tsx` files. I am planning to enhance the "home page" within the dashboard with a greeting and actions. Also, I've started the Hindi translations, building the dictionary for important terms.


**Solidifying the Deal**

I'm now thinking about the "ki" functionality and confirming that we're on the right track. My plan is to finalize the deal, ensuring we're delivering what's promised. Let's make it real.

entries.description: Fix horizontal sliding issue with snap behavior and add localization support.

payload.text: Please use Light background. . Refine the diary scanning and analysis feature to improve handwriting recognition accuracy, especially for diverse Indian regional scripts and common abbreviations.

**Analyzing Upcoming Changes**

I'm currently focused on the user's request for application changes. The most pressing items seem to be the shift to a lighter background and, more complexly, enhancing handwriting recognition across a spectrum of Indian regional scripts and abbreviations. I'm investigating how this can be handled through updates to the current system.


**Updating For Light Mode**

I'm now fully immersed in the light mode implementation. Specifically, I'm updating `index.html`, `App.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, `KanbanBoard.tsx`, and `Diary` components to ensure a seamless and vibrant "Indian" feel. In `services/gemini.ts`, I've refined the `DIARY_ANALYSIS_INSTRUCTION` to cover Indian scripts and shop abbreviations. I am working to ensure the cards are sliding smoothly and their layout is perfect for light mode.


**Developing Light Mode Components**

I'm now deep in the code, updating several components for light mode: `ChatAgent.tsx`, `BottomNav.tsx`, `CustomerManager.tsx`, and `CustomerLedger.tsx`. I am setting a white background with a subtle pattern, white cards with soft shadows, dark text, and vibrant accents. I'm also refining the model instructions within `services/gemini.ts` to handle Hinglish, Devanagari, and abbreviations for financial data, along with optimizing card sliding and layout within the Kanban board.

To enhance the **Sudarshan CRM Assistant**, I will transition the entire application to a vibrant, high-contrast **Light Theme** that feels professional and culturally resonant. I will also refine the **Gemini AI instructions** to specifically handle the nuances of Indian business handwriting (multiple scripts and abbreviations).

### Specification of Updates:
1.  **Visual Overhaul (Light Theme):**
    *   **Background:** Shifted from deep slate to a clean, off-white (`#fdfdfd`) with a subtle "Saffron and Green" accent pattern.
    *   **Typography:** Increased contrast. Headings in Slate-900, secondary text in Slate-600 for maximum readability in outdoor shop settings.
    *   **Vibrant Accents:** Using a "Tiranga" inspired palette (Indian Saffron, White, and India Green) for buttons and active states.
    *   **Glassmorphism:** Updated cards to a "Frosty White" look with soft shadows (`shadow-sm` to `shadow-xl`) instead of dark translucency.
2.  **Diary Scanning Optimization:**
    *   Updated the `DIARY_ANALYSIS_INSTRUCTION` to explicitly guide the AI on regional script variants (Devanagari, Gujarati, Bengali, etc.) and common ledger abbreviations like *'Kh.' (Kharcha)*, *'Bk.' (Bikri)*, *'Bal' (Balance)*, and *'Pmt' (Payment)*.
3.  **Mobile Polish:**
    *   Enhanced the **Kanban Board** with smoother horizontal snapping for better thumb-scrolling on small mobile screens.
    *   Updated the **Bottom Navigation** for a cleaner, modern light-mode look.

entries.description: Transition to a vibrant Light Theme with Indian cultural accents.
Refine handwriting recognition prompts for Indian scripts and abbreviations.
Update App container to support the Light Theme aesthetic.
Redesign Dashboard for Light Mode with high visibility.

payload.text: On the Customer Ledger screen, after a new transaction is added (Sale, Purchase, Expense), automatically update the customer's 'due_amount' based on the transaction type and amount. On the ALL screen, ensure all text is easily readable by increasing font sizes and contrast where necessary.

**Enhancing Ledger Functionality**

I've been focusing on the `CustomerLedger` component, specifically on making transaction additions instantly update the customer's `due_amount`. This should streamline the process considerably. Simultaneously, I'm working to increase the app's overall font size for improved readability.


**Refining App Readability**

I'm now prioritizing readability across the entire application, as the switch to a light theme warrants adjustments. I'm scrutinizing font sizes and contrast to ensure a comfortable user experience on every screen, including the `App.tsx` header, `BottomNav.tsx` labels, and components such as `Dashboard.tsx`, `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx`, `ChatAgent.tsx`, `CustomerManager.tsx`, `KanbanBoard.tsx`, and `VoiceAssistant.tsx`. My current focus is implementing font size, weight, and color changes in those components, and checking the existing implementation in `KanbanBoard.tsx`.


**Updating Due Amount Logic**

I'm presently refactoring the transaction types in `CustomerLedger` to better reflect their impact on the `due_amount`. I'm going to add `PAYMENT_RECEIVED` to the quick entry list, as it's essential for accurately tracking debt reduction.  The goal is to ensure the customer's due amount updates instantly, eliminating any manual adjustment. I want to replace `SALE`, `PURCHASE`, `EXPENSE` if possible with something clearer for the customer ledger.


**Improving Transaction Handling**

I'm now revising the transaction type definitions within `CustomerLedger` to better reflect their influence on the `due_amount`, by adding `PAYMENT_RECEIVED` to quick entry for clarity. `SALE` will increase, and `PAYMENT_RECEIVED` will decrease the `due_amount`. I'm evaluating how to handle other transaction types.


**Prioritizing Text Clarity**

I'm now focusing on standardizing font sizes across several components. The `App.tsx` header is getting attention; I'll scale "Sudarshan" to `text-2xl` and the tagline to `text-xs`.  I'm also increasing the `BottomNav.tsx` label size to `text-[10px]` for better accessibility. Additionally, I'm adjusting labels to `text-xs` in `Dashboard.tsx` and adjusting `KanbanBoard.tsx` text to `text-xs` and `text-[10px]`.

payload.text: Apply the edits shown in the screenshot(s). Imrpove the layout and text visibility

**Analyzing Visual Discrepancies**

I'm now zeroing in on a visual inconsistency. My internal analysis flagged a discrepancy: the "Kaam Ki List" (Kanban Board) has a dark background in the screenshot provided. This conflicts with a prior change that made it light. I need to verify why, especially given the user's latest directives.


**Interpreting User Intent**

I'm now struggling to decipher the user's true intent. They explicitly requested a light background, yet the provided screenshot displays a dark one with elements. It seems the screenshot is to fix the layout within the *dark* theme (which had a blue "Update" button). Their earlier prompts regarding reminders and a dark theme screenshot add to the complexity of the interpretation.


**Deciphering Visual Clues**

Okay, so the latest screenshot clearly displays a dark theme, despite the user's previous preference for light. This dark mode visual is in line with the update button also being blue instead of white. The user circled a specific card and column with a price tag, along with mentioning cards not sliding.  I need to reconcile these conflicting clues and understand whether this dark theme is *implied* to be changed or if is meant to remain in place - or only to focus on layout as suggested. The optimization for mobile screens still needs to be addressed, per the previous prompt.


**Focusing on Light Mode**

I'm now focusing on how the Kanban board components should look and behave in light mode, as requested. The screenshot provides valuable clues, showing a "Self" badge, an "Update" button, and a dark column background. My analysis now zeroes in on card visibility and horizontal sliding issues. I plan to refine `KanbanBoard.tsx` and other related components to improve both the light mode visibility and the responsiveness.


**Reconciling Visual Styles**

I'm now integrating the user's latest directives. I'm focusing on the Home Page and Hindi option, per their initial requests. I'm also addressing visibility issues by enlarging fonts and increasing contrast for status labels. I am also working on re-applying the visual styling from a recent dark-themed screenshot (card layout, full-width update button, pop-out card border), but I must reconcile that with the earlier light theme request. The 'Ramesh Halwai' card display is a critical component here.


**Updating Kanban Layout**

I'm now prioritizing the `KanbanBoard` component, aiming for a premium layout that directly reflects the circled card's structure in the provided screenshot, including the bubble-style column headers and the distinct "Update" button style. My plan encompasses refining `KanbanBoard.tsx` and verifying the "sliding" functionality with `snap-center`, addressing horizontal scroll and mobile optimization in the process. I'll also boost font sizes globally and check the "Hindi" option. Finally, I will address the issue of the "Update" button, which seems related to card movement.


**Implementing the Kanban Board**

I am now focused on the exact Kanban layout from the screenshot. I need to make the columns more distinct, and the `moveLead` functionality must be reactive within `KanbanBoard.tsx`. I'll address the specific card details from the screenshot: the "Self" badge, amount pill, description below, and the "Update" button at the bottom of each card. The column headers, like "NAYE LOG (LEADS)," will be styled as horizontal pills, and the handwriting recognition prompt will be refined to be specific about MSME abbreviations.

**Reviewing Visual Edits**

I'm now zeroing in on the visual edits. The primary focus is the `KanbanBoard`, or "Kaam Ki List" as it's known, based on that provided light theme screenshot. I'm keen to ensure any changes align with your expectations and refine the application's aesthetic based on your expertise.


**Addressing Text Clarity & UX**

I'm now diving into the user experience within the Kanban board, including text legibility. Subtle animations for card interactions are on the agenda. Next, I'm verifying spacing consistency. A scan and report generation feature is also being added for the user's convenience. The details of cards are now clear. The user has asked for a dark background but provided screenshots with a light board, and then a dark background. So it will be dark, with blue highlights.


**Analyzing Current Screenshot Style**

I'm now revisiting the screenshot to extract design elements. It's a "Kaam Ki List" Kanban board with "New Leads," which provides context. I'll translate the card styling within, including the orange/blue border. The focus is now on the card style's finer points based on this specific reference.


**Integrating UI Elements**

I'm now integrating the "Update" button and pill headers within the card component. I'm also adding "Scan" and "Report" buttons, either to the header or as a floating action group. A global report action is also a possibility. The theme will now be light, but with the structural layout elements from the provided screenshot.


**Prioritizing UI Elements**

I'm now prioritizing the UI elements. "Scan" and "Report" will likely go in the `Dashboard`. I'm also now considering adding a dedicated "Reports" section, or an action within the dashboard, powered by Gemini. Further, I'm verifying the voice assistant's design, noting its dark circle on a light background from the screenshot, and I will be working on text legibility, starting with increased contrast.


**Refining Report Generation**

I'm currently working to integrate the "Scan" function, which is already present as `DiaryAnalyzer`, making sure it's linked correctly. I'll include "Scan" in both the header of the `KanbanBoard` and `Dashboard`. Moreover, I'm adding a "Reports" button to generate a "Business Health Report" in the `Dashboard` using Gemini. I'm also updating `KanbanBoard.tsx`, `App.tsx`, and `BottomNav.tsx` to add these features and improve their accessibility. I've noted that the cards will follow the styling from the existing screenshot.

payload.text: Add a button to the Diary Analyzer screen that allows users to record audio and have it transcribed to initiate the diary image analysis. This should integrate with the existing `analyzeDiaryImage` function by passing the transcribed text alongside a placeholder for the image.

**Initiating Audio Integration**

I'm currently focused on the `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` file. My immediate goal is to establish state variables for audio recording (`recording`) and the transcribed text (`transcription`). This is a foundational step for implementing the user's audio-to-image analysis pipeline.


**Developing Audio Transcription Flow**

I'm now zeroing in on integrating the audio transcription. I've decided to use `ai.models.generateContent` with the audio modality for transcription and integrate it within the existing analysis flow. I'll need to update the `analyzeDiaryImage` function in `services/gemini.ts` to handle optional text instructions from the transcription. The plan is to add a "Voice Command" button to the image preview screen and include a recording state with a pulse animation.


**Refining Voice Interaction**

I'm now refining the audio integration, specifically how to initiate the image analysis with voice. I'm focusing on updating the `analyzeDiaryImage` function in `services/gemini.ts` to accept a `prompt` parameter for transcribed text, which will be passed alongside the image. I will add a recording button to trigger the audio analysis, making it an alternative "initiation" method.

payload.text:
{ Analyze each point , one by one and proceed to complete every instruction}-
Add a button to the Diary Analyzer screen that allows users to record audio and have it transcribed to initiate the diary image analysis. This should integrate with the existing analyzeDiaryImage function by passing the transcribed text alongside a placeholder for the image.
Add a 'Scan and Generate Report' button to the Diary Analyzer screen. This feature should take the analyzed diary data and generate a downloadable report.
On the Customer Ledger screen, after a new transaction is added (Sale, Purchase, Expense), automatically update the customer's 'due_amount' based on the transaction type and amount.

**Adding Audio Input**

I'm currently focused on integrating the record audio functionality into the CRM assistant. My primary aim is to seamlessly add a button for audio recording. I'm considering various implementation methods, prioritizing a clean, user-friendly interface as dictated by the implicit visual guide, and mindful of efficient audio handling.


**Implementing New Features**

I've made progress on several features. I'm adding voice control for diary analysis using `transcribeAudio`, which will feed into the image analysis. I'm also adding a "Scan and Generate Report" button. Additionally, I'm working to automatically update `due_amount` after each transaction, currently reviewing and updating `handleAddTx` for various transaction types. Finally, I'm increasing font sizes and contrast for better readability across the app.


**Refining Transaction Logic**

I'm currently updating the `CustomerLedger` transaction logic to accurately reflect sales, payments, and expenses. I'm streamlining the calculations for due amounts, making sure they correctly increase with sales and decrease with payments and purchases. I've focused on clarity, and am prioritizing consistency in how transactions are handled.


**Updating Transaction Logic**

I'm now revising the transaction types in `types.ts` and refining the logic within `CustomerLedger.tsx`. I've updated how different transaction types affect the `due_amount` calculation. I'm focusing on ensuring accurate and intuitive handling of sales, payments, purchases, and expenses within the customer ledger, and making sure that sales increase the `due_amount` while purchases, expenses, and payments decrease it. I'm starting the code changes outlined, now.


**Revising UI Integration**

I'm now integrating the updated transaction handling with UI elements across the app, ensuring seamless data flow and user interaction. I'm focusing on consistency in how the updated logic affects the user interface, incorporating readability improvements like increased font sizes and weights in `App.tsx`, `Dashboard.tsx`, and `BottomNav.tsx` to enhance clarity across all screens. I'm also ensuring the Diary Analyzer screen has the audio button and integrates with the report generation.


**Enhancing Screen Readability**

I am now focusing on the "ALL" screen and prioritizing text readability across all elements. I'm exploring adjustments to font sizes, weights, and color contrast, aiming to enhance the visual clarity of the text for improved user comprehension. I am going to revisit all the existing screens and improve their readability.


**Expanding Transaction Logic**

I'm now expanding the transaction logic within `CustomerLedger.tsx` to include `PURCHASE` and `EXPENSE` types, ensuring `due_amount` calculations are comprehensive. I'm also revising font sizes and weights in `Dashboard.tsx`, improving readability in the "ALL" screen and other crucial components. I'm ensuring all transaction types affect the `due_amount` appropriately, to deliver precise ledger updates.

To satisfy your request, I will update the application to ensure a swift, high-contrast, and highly readable experience tailored for Indian business owners. 

### Specification of Updates:

1.  **Diary Analyzer Enhancements**:
    *   **Voice Integration**: The prominent microphone button now allows for recording audio commands (e.g., "Check total sales for October") which are transcribed and used as context for the AI image analysis.
    *   **Scan & Report Feature**: A new "Scan & Generate Report" workflow is added. After analysis, a dedicated action button allows the immediate generation of a formatted business summary report (downloadable as a text file).
2.  **Intelligent Ledger Automation**:
    *   **Auto-Calculations**: In `CustomerLedger.tsx`, the logic is updated to automatically adjust the `due_amount` for all transaction types:
        *   **Sale**: Increases debt (+).
        *   **Payment Received**: Decreases debt (-).
        *   **Purchase**: Decreases debt (-) (assuming a credit toward their balance).
        *   **Expense**: Decreases debt (-) (standardizing credits).
3.  **Readability & Accessibility Overhaul**:
    *   **Font Scaling**: Increased base font sizes across all screens (Dashboard, Ledger, Tasks).
    *   **High Contrast**: Darker text colors (`text-slate-900` instead of `slate-700`) and bolder font weights (`font-black`, `font-extrabold`) for critical data points like amounts and customer names.
    *   **Visual Hierarchy**: Enhanced padding and shadow depth to separate card elements clearly on mobile screens.

Automatically generate a diary analysis report upon successful analysis, offering it for download without requiring an explicit 'Scan & Generate Report' button click.

**Automating Diary Analysis**

My focus is shifting towards automating the `DiaryAnalyzer`. The voice recording transcription is already in place; now, I'm working on enabling automatic triggering of the analysis whenever new transcriptions become available. This will streamline the process and minimize manual intervention.


**Refining Automated Triggers**

I've been improving the automatic analysis trigger. It's designed to start when an image is selected *and* text is present, whether from transcription or manual input. I'm focusing on ensuring reports are generated upon analysis, making a "Download Ready" notification or button immediately available without the need for an explicit generation click, and eliminating an automated download.


**Streamlining the User Experience**

I'm removing the "Start Analysis" button for a zero-click experience. Analysis triggers automatically upon image upload or voice transcription completion, provided an image is available. Manual text input will either trigger analysis on pressing enter, or perhaps via a small "Run" button if the image is not ready. I'm also focusing on integrating report data preparation to immediately show a download link once analysis completes, and styling all new elements for maximum readability, following the "Munim Ji" senior frontend engineer style.

payload.text:
Automatically generate a diary analysis report upon successful analysis, offering it for download without requiring an explicit 'Scan & Generate Report' button click.
Add a button to the Diary Analyzer screen that allows users to record audio and have it transcribed to initiate the diary image analysis. This should integrate with the existing analyzeDiaryImage function by passing the transcribed text alongside a placeholder for the image.
Add a 'Scan and Generate Report' button to the Diary Analyzer screen. This feature should take the analyzed diary data and generate a downloadable report.

**Considering TTS Integration**

I'm exploring how to integrate the `gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts` model for Text-to-Speech into the Sudarshan CRM Assistant. The existing `speakHindi` function in `services/gemini.ts` offers a starting point. I'm focusing on adapting this to use the new TTS model and how to provide the desired functionality without disrupting the current structure.


**Analyzing Component Integrations**

I'm now analyzing where `speakHindi` is used, specifically within `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` and `ChatAgent.tsx`. I have identified several feature requests concerning diary analysis: auto-generation of reports, audio transcription capabilities, a "Scan and Generate Report" button (which I'll interpret as report availability), refined analysis triggering, and manual transcription input. I also noticed `speakHindi` already exists, and the user's focus on the `gemini-2.5` model requires further clarification.


**Refining Feature Implementation**

I'm now refining the `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` component based on specific feature requests. I've re-reviewed the guidelines for `gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts` and confirmed the correct methods: `response.text` for text extraction, and `Modality.AUDIO` with `speechConfig` for TTS, which yields raw PCM data. My `speakHindi` implementation appears sound. I'm focusing on integrating features like a record audio button, auto-report generation, and auto-analysis triggers based on image or voice input, along with text input for manual transcription. I'm leveraging the existing `triggerAnalysis` function and `useEffect` hooks in `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` to streamline the implementation.


**Implementing Automated Reporting**

I'm now integrating automated report generation after analysis, eliminating the need for a separate button. I'll trigger the report download or UI immediately post-analysis. I'm also reviewing `services/gemini.ts` for TTS implementation and confirming the usage of `gemini-2.5-flash-preview-tts` for the core TTS, ensuring it's not being confused with other models.


**Clarifying Automated Reporting**

I'm now clarifying the auto-report generation and confirming report availability post-analysis. I'm focusing on the auto-trigger flow: image upload, voice transcription, then analysis trigger. I'll modify `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` to display a prompt or link for report access when results become available, avoiding automatic downloads. I'm reviewing `services/gemini.ts` to confirm TTS model and parameter use.

payload.text: Integrate a feature on the Diary Analyzer screen that allows users to upload an image of their handwritten diary entries. The AI should then analyze the image to extract transactions, calculate totals (sales, purchases, expenses), and provide a Hindi summary and 5 actionable business improvement tips.

**Refining the Analyzer**

I'm currently focused on the `DiaryAnalyzer` screen and its refinement. My primary goal is to integrate a prominent microphone button. This button will be crucial for capturing audio input, leveraging the Gemini model for transcription, and utilizing the transcribed text to enhance the image analysis process. I anticipate this new feature will provide rich context for analysis.


**Automating Report Generation**

I've made progress on automating the report generation. Now, the `useEffect` hook in `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` will automatically trigger analysis when an image or transcription is available. I've also added a `textarea` for manual instructions and ensured the report is offered promptly upon successful analysis, without requiring a separate button click. I'll automatically generate the report upon analysis and potentially offer a `speakHindi` option.


**Reviewing DiaryAnalyzer.tsx**

I'm now carefully reviewing the existing `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` code. While many features are already present, I'm focusing on incorporating the user's specific new requests. I'm prioritizing the implementation of a robust audio recording button for the user to be able to upload a transcript along with the image. I will ensure a clear "Download Report" option appears upon analysis completion, streamlining the report download process without requiring additional button clicks.


**Streamlining Analysis Triggers**

I'm now implementing the automatic analysis trigger. I've removed the redundant "Start Analysis" button. I'll use `useEffect` to trigger analysis based on image upload or manual transcription input. I'm also adding a `textarea` field for manual transcription. I am going to have a "Download Report" card that will appear in the results section with high-contrast, bold fonts.


**Integrating Manual Text Input**

I'm now integrating a manual text input field into `DiaryAnalyzer.tsx` for entering transcribed text. This is a recent development. I'm focusing on ensuring it works seamlessly alongside image uploads, triggering analysis automatically. I will also incorporate the auto-run analysis trigger, and a download report button with an auto-speak call.
